title: On The Death Of Leopold, King Of The Belgians[1]
content:
A King is dead!    Another master mind
         Is summoned from the world-wide council hall.
    Ah, for some seer, to say what links behind - 
         To read the mystic writing on the wall!

    Be still, fond man:    nor ask thy fate to know.
         Face bravely what each God-sent moment brings.
    Above thee rules in love, through weal and woe,
         Guiding thy kings and thee, the King of kings.

    Windsor Castle,
         November 10, 1865.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles Kingsley, titled "On The Death Of Leopold, King Of The Belgians[1]", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
